This document contains examples of every citation type supported by jetcite,
including forgiving variants and edge cases.

== U.S. CONSTITUTION ==

The First Amendment protects free speech. U.S. Const. amend. I. The Due
Process Clause, U.S. Const. amend. XIV, § 1, applies to the states. See
also Article III of the United States Constitution (judicial power).
The Establishment Clause is found at U.S. Const. amend. I. The Supremacy
Clause appears at U.S. Const. art. VI, § 2. Amendment X to the United
States Constitution reserves powers to the states.

== ND CONSTITUTION ==

The right to bear arms is protected by N.D. Const. art. I, § 20. See
also Article XI, section 6 of the North Dakota Constitution (legislative
authority). The provision at ND Const. art. I, § 12 guarantees due process.

== FEDERAL STATUTES ==

Section 1983 of Title 42, cited as 42 U.S.C. § 1983, creates a private
right of action. See also 28 U.S.C. § 1331 (federal question jurisdiction)
and 42 USC § 1988 (attorney fees). The range provision at 42 U.S.C.
§§ 1983-1985 covers related claims. Subsections matter: 18 U.S.C.
§ 922(g)(1) restricts firearm possession.

== CODE OF FEDERAL REGULATIONS ==

OSHA standards appear at 29 C.F.R. § 1910.1200. Environmental regulations
are found at 40 CFR 122.41. The flexible form 29 CFR § 1910.1200(a)(2) is
also recognized.

== FEDERAL RULES ==

Summary judgment is governed by Fed. R. Civ. P. 56. A motion to dismiss
for failure to state a claim is brought under Fed. R. Civ. P. 12(b)(6),
sometimes abbreviated FRCP 12(b)(6). The hearsay rule is Fed. R. Evid.
801. Appellate briefs must comply with Fed. R. App. P. 28. Criminal
defendants have rights under Fed. R. Crim. P. 29. Bankruptcy matters are
governed by Fed. R. Bankr. P. 7001. The abbreviation FRE 403 excludes
prejudicial evidence.

== NDCC (NORTH DAKOTA CENTURY CODE) ==

The rules of construction appear at N.D.C.C. § 1-02-13. See also
NDCC § 12.1-32-01 (sentencing guidelines) and section 14-02-05, N.D.C.C.
(marriage requirements). Chapter references like NDCC ch. 14-02 point to
an entire chapter. Decimal titles are common: N.D.C.C. § 12.1-32-01.1
includes subsection decimals.

== NDAC (NORTH DAKOTA ADMINISTRATIVE CODE) ==

Administrative rules appear at N.D.A.C. § 43-02-05-01. Chapter references
use N.D.A.C. ch. 43-02-05. The reverse form 43-02-05-01, N.D. Admin.
Code is also recognized.

== ND COURT RULES ==

Civil procedure: N.D.R.Civ.P. Rule 56 (summary judgment).
Criminal procedure: N.D.R.Crim.P. 29 (motion for judgment of acquittal).
Appellate procedure: N.D.R.App.P. 28 (briefs).
Evidence: N.D.R.Ev. 803 (hearsay exceptions), Rule 403, N.D.R.Ev.
Rules of court: N.D.R.Ct. 8.3.1 (three-part) and N.D.R.Ct. 11.10 (two-part).
Supreme Court admin: N.D. Sup. Ct. Admin. R. 1.2 and N.D. Sup. Ct. Admin. R. 5.
Professional conduct: N.D.R. Prof. Conduct 1.1.
Lawyer discipline: N.D.R. Lawyer Discipl. 3.2.
Judicial conduct: N.D. Code Jud. Conduct 2.3 and Canon 1 : Rule 2.3, N.D. Code Jud. Conduct.
Juvenile procedure: N.D.R. Juv. P. 5.1.
Continuing legal ed: N.D.R. Continuing Legal Ed. 3.
Admission to practice: N.D. Admission to Practice R. 2.1 and N.D. Admission to Practice R. 4.
Lawyer sanctions: N.D. Stds. Imposing Lawyer Sanctions 1.
Local rules: Local Rule 100-1.
Procedural rules: N.D.R. Proc. R. 5.
Local court: N.D.R. Local Ct. P.R. 3.
Judicial conduct commission: N.D.R. Jud. Conduct Commission 2.5 and N.D.R. Jud. Conduct Commission 7.

== MEDIUM-NEUTRAL STATE CITATIONS ==

North Dakota: 2024 ND 156, ¶ 12 (with pinpoint).
Ohio: 2018-Ohio-3237.
New Mexico: 2009-NMSC-006 and 2009-NMCA-123.
Illinois: 2011 IL 102345.
Colorado: 2019 CO 44.
Maine: 2011 ME 24.
Montana: 1998 MT 12.
South Dakota: 2013 S.D. 54.
Utah: 1999 UT 16.
Vermont: 2001 VT 1.
Wisconsin: 2000 WI 14.
Wyoming: 2001 WY 12.
Oklahoma: 2006 OK 24.
Arkansas: 2017 Ark. App. 605.
North Carolina: 2021-NCSC-57.
Pennsylvania: 1999 PA Super 1.
Puerto Rico: 2015 TSPR 148.

== REGIONAL REPORTERS ==

Northwest: 585 N.W.2d 123 and 993 N.W.3d 374 and 100 N.W. 500.
Atlantic: 200 A.3d 400 and 150 A.2d 300.
Northeast: 175 N.E.3d 250.
Southeast: 300 S.E.2d 100.
Southern: 400 So. 3d 200 and 250 So. 2d 150.
Southwest: 500 S.W.3d 300.
Pacific: 150 P.3d 200.
Malformed: 585 NW2d 123 (no periods).

== STATE-SPECIFIC REPORTERS ==

North Dakota Reports: 50 N.D. 123 (historical, vols 1-79).
California: 50 Cal. 4th 300 and 75 Cal. Rptr. 3d 200.
New York: 35 N.Y.3d 100 and 80 N.Y.S.3d 50.
Ohio: 160 Ohio St. 3d 200.
Illinois: 200 Ill. 2d 100 and 150 Ill. Dec. 50.
Washington: 180 Wash. 2d 300 and 90 Wash. App. 2d 100.
Other states: 200 Conn. 300 and 150 Ga. 200.

== FEDERAL CASE REPORTERS ==

U.S. Reports: 505 U.S. 377 (via Justia).
Supreme Court Reporter: 140 S. Ct. 1731.
Federal Reporter: 400 F.3d 500 and 50 F.4th 100 and 200 F.2d 300.
Federal Supplement: 500 F. Supp. 3d 100 and 300 F. Supp. 2d 200.
Lawyers' Edition: 120 L. Ed. 2d 500.
Bankruptcy Reporter: 300 B.R. 50.
Federal Rules Decisions: 200 F.R.D. 100.
Federal Claims: 100 Fed. Cl. 50.
Military Justice: 75 M.J. 200.
Veterans Appeals: 30 Vet. App. 100.
Tax Court: 150 T.C. 50.
Federal Appendix: 600 F. App'x 100.

== PARALLEL CITATIONS ==

Modern ND: 2024 ND 156, 10 N.W.3d 500.
Older ND: 585 N.W.2d 123, 2000 ND 45.
Historical ND: 50 N.D. 123, 195 N.W. 500.
With pinpoint: 2020 ND 100, ¶ 15, 950 N.W.2d 200.
